selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 3.9% and 20-day momentum is 26.3%.
Trend quality is supported by 70% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 12.1% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 2.4%, downside volatility near 0.8%, and a recent max drawdown of 2.7%.
Volume confirmation is -8.8% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 1.5% versus SPY over 5 days and 3.7% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.75, confidence 0.74, and 0.8 weighted articles across 2 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.75, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 2 sources, 0.8 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.78, average source quality 0.79, confidence 0.74.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC -0.03).
Overall model score is 0.201 with high confidence.

news_evidence.title: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) Raises Over $17 billion Through Euro-denominated Bonds
news_evidence.llm_reason: Direct, material financing announcement for Alphabet: ~€9bn (+ other issuance) to fund AI investments. This is company-specific and could influence near-term sentiment and liquidity expectations. Not likely to radically change fundamentals overnight, but meaningful enough to move the stock in the 1–10 day window if picked up by markets.

news_evidence.title: Global Market Pulse: ByteDance Ramps AI Spending to 200B Yuan Amid Middle East Escalation and Gold Supply Crunch
news_evidence.llm_reason: ByteDance’s large increase in AI capex is sector-relevant and could raise competitive concerns and sentiment around AI incumbents. Effect on Alphabet over the next 1–10 days is likely modest (sector/competitive signal rather than direct company action), so this is a moderate short-term negative influence.
